title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Behavior Brief: Performance Measurement
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js performance measurement increment in a tested and release-ready JavaScript application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck.
Enforce formatting, linting, type-aware JavaScript checks, tests, dead-code detection, and dependency audit in local and CI gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.

steps.title: Predict Performance Measurement
steps.instruction:
theory case — A release candidate passes the familiar 4-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Performance Measurement contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck.

options.text: Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Shorter source code is necessarily faster JavaScript. This does not resolve predict performance measurement.

For predict performance measurement, apply Performance Measurement without its stated preconditions, omit the a tested and release-ready JavaScript application trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Performance Measurement for a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.

content.text: Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Trace concrete runtime values, bindings, receiver, mutation, and queued work in order; assert the observable result, then change one input or event and seek the first causal divergence. Apply that move specifically to Performance Measurement while producing a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.

steps.title: Defend the Performance Measurement model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck.
